summary: Linked Sheik neutral special (`ftSk_SpecialN.c`) at 3.87 KB / 100%, replacing the assembly object with the C object and deleting `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialN.s`. The PR cleaned the C implementation by adding local helpers for duplicated ground/air enter, IASA, collision, and damage-callback logic; moved internal helpers/data to local scope; updated Sheik fighter-var field layout names/types; fixed item needle prototypes to return/use `Item_GObj*`; and adjusted splits/symbols/tooling metadata for the now-linked file.
what_changed: Removed the 1213-line assembly source `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialN.s` and switched the build from `asm/.../ftSk_SpecialN.s.o` to `src/.../ftSk_SpecialN.c.o`.

Reworked `ftSk_SpecialN.c` into a matching C implementation for Sheik needle charge/shoot/cancel/end states, including `ftSk_SpecialN_80111FBC`, ground/air enter routines, start/loop/cancel/end anim/IASA/phys/coll routines, and the local needle shooting callback.
Renamed/limited internal symbols: `ftSk_SpecialN_801120D4` became local, `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles` became local `shootNeedles`, and the needle vertical offset table became local `needleYPosScale`.
Added helper functions such as `setDmgCallbacks`, `clearDmgCallbacks`, `doEnter`, `doIasa`, and `doColl` to share duplicated logic between ground and air variants while still matching.

Updated Sheik fighter variable struct offsets in `types.h` from absolute-offset-style names (`x222C`, `x2230`, `x2234`, `x2238`, `x2268`) to struct-relative names (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x3C`) and typed `x4` as `Item_GObj*`; changed `mv.sk.specialn.x4` from `s32` to `bool` and `x0` to `enum_t`.
Updated existing Sheik SpecialS and Init code to use the renamed `fp->fv.sk` fields, preserving offsets while making the shared fighter-vars layout consistent.
Corrected held-needle item prototypes in `itseakneedleheld.h` to use `Item_GObj*` return/argument types instead of `Fighter_GObj*`/raw `u32` for item object pointers.
Adjusted `config/GALE01/splits.txt` and `symbols.txt` for updated `.data`/`.sdata2` boundaries, local scopes, symbol names, and anonymous jump-table/local data symbols.
Changed `tools/report_score.py` so a 100% result prints `Link` instead of `Match`, reflected by the PR title/body report.
Touched `grtclink.c` and stage split boundaries with a small data formatting/scope change: made the callback table static and expressed `0xC0000000` as `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)`.
smart_moves: Factored repeated ground/air motion transitions into inline helpers (`doEnter`, `doIasa`, `doColl`) instead of maintaining near-duplicate functions, a useful pattern when the compiler still accepts inlining without disturbing the match.

Used typed enums/constants for motion states (`ftSk_MS_SpecialNStart`, `ftSk_MS_SpecialAirNLoop`, etc.), flags (`Ft_MF_None`), inputs (`HSD_PAD_B`, `HSD_PAD_LR`), and booleans/NULLs rather than raw literals where possible.
Kept explicit `PAD_STACK(...)` in functions needing stack-shape preservation, showing a controlled way to improve readability while retaining exact codegen.
Moved implementation-only helpers/data out of the public header and symbol global scope, reducing exported surface while matching the original object layout.

Fixed item API typing at the header level (`Item_GObj*`) so the decompiled code could express needle item creation/ownership accurately instead of relying on loose `Fighter_GObj*`/integer placeholders.
Preserved exact object layout by updating splits and symbol scopes alongside the source/build switch, including reduced `.data` and `.sdata2` ends for `ftSk_SpecialN.c`.
decomp_lessons: When linking a file, update all three integration points together: delete/stop using the asm object, mark the C object as `Matching` in `configure.py`, and replace the asm object path in `obj_files.mk`.
For fighter-var structs, prefer struct-relative field names when the struct base is already offset in the parent `Fighter`; update all users together to avoid mixed absolute/relative naming.
Internal callback functions used only through function pointers do not need to remain public header declarations; local static functions plus symbol scope updates can still match.
Ground/air variants that differ mostly by motion-state IDs can often be expressed with a small shared helper parameterized by the motion IDs.
Typed object-pointer prototypes are important for matching and maintainability: item-spawning functions should return `Item_GObj*`, not generic fighter object pointers.
Use `PAD_STACK` only where evidence from matching requires stack padding; this PR shows it coexisting with cleaner local-variable scoping.
Splits and symbols may shift after C data is expressed more accurately, especially local arrays, jump tables, and sdata constants; validate data boundaries as part of link PRs.
naming_conventions: Sheik file/symbol prefix remains `ftSk_`; neutral special state handlers use `ftSk_SpecialN...` and air variants use `ftSk_SpecialAirN...`.
Implementation-only helper names in this PR are lowercase descriptive names such as `shootNeedles`, `setDmgCallbacks`, `clearDmgCallbacks`, `doEnter`, `doIasa`, and `doColl`.
Data table `ftSk_SpecialN_803CC598` was renamed to descriptive local `needleYPosScale`.
Sheik fighter-vars fields were normalized from absolute offset names to relative names: `x222C` -> `x0`, `x2230` -> `x4`, `x2234` -> `x8`, `x2238` -> `xC`, `x2268` -> `x3C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the assembly file only after every listed function in `ftSk_SpecialN.c` reported 100.00% / score 0.
Used local inline helpers to reduce duplicated C while apparently preserving generated code for multiple state handlers.
Inserted `PAD_STACK(4 * 1)`, `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)`, and `PAD_STACK(4 * 8)` in selected routines to preserve stack layout.
Used scoped blocks around temporaries such as `Item_GObj* item_gobj`, `float y_scale`, and repeated `Fighter* fp` declarations to shape lifetimes/codegen.
Represented motion flags as a static local bitmask expression `(1 << 7) | (1 << 12) | ...` instead of a raw `0x0C4C5080U`, with a `@todo Named flags` note.
Changed literals to equivalent decimal or semantic forms (`79`, `23`, `80`, `100`, `1283`, `270134`, `270140`, `HSD_PAD_B`, `HSD_PAD_LR`) where this did not break matching.
Updated symbol scopes and anonymous local data names (`@279`, `@328`) to reflect compiler-emitted local jump/data tables.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice; no human reviewer guidance can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftSeak_FighterVars` fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, or `x3C` to semantic names.
Search for other fighter special PRs that use `doEnter`/`doIasa`/`doColl`-style inline helpers to match ground and air variants.
Audit remaining `itseakneedleheld` call sites for `Item_GObj*` typing consistency.
Look for later work replacing the `@todo Named flags` bitmask in Sheik collision transitions with named motion flags.
